Hi! So Nice To Meet You!,"Hi! So Nice To Meet You! Hello new friend! We are Elsie & Emma and it's so nice to meet you. Welcome to the A Beautiful Mess newsletter! We are sisters and we've been blogging for over 15 years. Our site is a place for creative people, like you! We share a huge mi Hello new friend! We are Elsie & Emma and it's so nice to meet you. Welcome to the A Beautiful Mess newsletter! We are sisters and we've been blogging for over 15 years. Our site is a place for creative people, like you! We share a huge mix of easy recipes, crafts, and home decorating ideas. We grew up with an art teacher mother who taught us that staying home and making stuff was the path to a happy life, and we've never looked back. (Although we have been known to leave our houses on occasion.) We hope to inspire you to make stuff—nothing would make us happier! Since we're just getting to know you, here is a mix of some of our most popular blog posts in different categories you might enjoy: Do you like crafting? Probably our most made and most copied craft post of all time are these marbled clay ring dishes that we posted back in 2014. Second most popular tutorial? How to make your own bath bombs. You can see our entire craft archive here. We have something for everyone, like sewing tutorials, jewelry making, clay projects, scrapbooking, and tons and tons of garlands and wreaths. Lol. Do you love home decor? We have hundreds of DIY projects on our site like this popular built-in bookcase hack. We also share lots of personal ideas and advice like this post about when an open floor plan is too open. We love hearing what our readers think, too! Are you into cooking or baking? We are! Here are a bunch of our best cookie recipes and easy no-bake desserts. And if you need something for dinner tonight, check out these 30-minute meal ideas. We also have a podcast. Our listeners say it's like catching up with an old friend. Here are a few episodes you might want to try if you want to get to know us:Elsie's Life Map and Emma's Life Map. We both grew up conservative evangelicals and feel pretty different about that today. Elsie shares more about this topic here, and you can read Emma's point of view here. But, we mostly share about things like decor trends and goal setting. It's a mix! There are three great ways to stay in touch with us. First, read our blog (which we update daily.) Next, subscribe to our podcast. We have new episodes every Monday. And, you can follow us on Instagram. We'd like to hear about you and things that you're interested in! Please hit reply and tell us three things about yourself, including your current favorite hobby. xo. Elsie & Emma 731 S. Pickwick AvenueSpringfield, MO 65802, United States Don’t want these emails anymore? Mercury Language Documentation Improvement Suggestion,"Hi Aetheros Support,

I've been working with Mercury for the past few months and have been really impressed with how it streamlines API development. However, I noticed a few areas in the documentation that I believe could be improved for clarity and completeness.
Specifically, I think it would be beneficial to:
1. **Expand on the section about error handling in Mercury.** A more detailed explanation of common error types and how to implement robust error handling would be incredibly helpful.
2. **Provide more practical examples of using IAM roles within Mercury code.** While the basic concepts are covered, having more real-world scenarios illustrated would greatly enhance understanding.
I'm confident that these additions would make the Mercury documentation even more valuable to developers like myself.
Thank you for your time and consideration.
Isiah Wuckert",0,0,1,Legitimate email from a user providing constructive feedback,"{

Concerns Over Medical Data Security Breach,"Greeting Customer Support Team,

I am contacting you to report an urgent problem with our medical data storage system. There has been an unintended data breach, and we are worried about the possible repercussions.
Following a preliminary investigation, it seems the breach may have resulted from a weak firewall setup. We attempted to address the issue by rebooting the firewall and installing current Ubuntu updates, but the problem still exists, and we urgently need your help to rectify it.
Could you please guide us on how to carry forward the investigation and suggest necessary measures to avoid similar situations in the future?
We are grateful for your swift action on this matter and await your response.

Best regards,

Statement of Account,"eStatement Dear Customer If you are reading this message, your registration is complete and we have confirmed your chosen e-mail address. Your eStatament is ready for viewing. simply login to the Absa App and Absa Online Banking to view,dowload or print your statement. LOGIN Guidelines Electronic statement delivery gives you access to your statements with no extra charge. If you choose electronic statements and then decide to change your e-mail address or go back to printed statements, please logon to Absa Online and follow the prompts or contact any Absa branch. Statements are available in English or Afrikaans. Absa only sends statements to the e-mail addresses you have specified. It is your responsibility to ensure that no unauthorised people have access to your e-mail or your password. If an unauthorised person accesses your e-mail or your password, Absa is not responsible for any loss or damage suffered as a result. You will receive the electronic statements because you have accepted the terms and conditions thereof. If you would like to view them again, please go to absa.co.za/estatementsterms For more information, please contact officialemail@absa.co.za or the call centre on 0860 111 123.
